adaptation_focus: adaptive capacity assessment and crop suitability adaptation planning
indicators_measured: crop suitability change; adaptive capacity index; vulnerability index; water access; education; roads density; loans; non-farm employment
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Integrates climate suitability and socioeconomic adaptive capacity at municipality scale. Limitation: Crop suitability does not directly measure productivity and several indicators rely on subjective weighting.
lessons_learned: Adaptation planning should combine geographic prioritization with adaptive-capacity analysis. Areas with weak basic services and high dependence on subsistence crops may require transformational adaptation.

adaptation_focus: participatory agroecology and livelihood diversification for climate adaptation
indicators_measured: HFIAS food security score; dietary diversity; intercropping; compost use; legume diversification; household assets
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Combines longitudinal surveys with participatory farmer experimentation and qualitative methods. Limitation: No control group due to participatory-action design.
lessons_learned: Participatory agroecology combined with attention to gender and social equity can improve food security and dietary diversity among vulnerable households.

adaptation_focus: livelihood-based adaptation strategies to climate risks
indicators_measured: livelihood adaptation index; adaptation strategies; food shortage months; farmer group membership; credit access
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Introduces weighted livelihood-based adaptation index and attitude measurement framework. Limitation: Adaptation behavior inferred primarily from survey responses.
lessons_learned: Reducing food insecurity and improving access to groups, credit, and climate information can enhance adaptation to climate risks.

indicators_measured: economic resources; social capital; training; technology; infrastructure; institutional support
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Develops region-specific adaptive capacity framework using expert validation. Limitation: Results derived from limited communities and indicator weighting may be context-specific.
lessons_learned: Women and farmers with limited education showed lower adaptive capacity, highlighting the need for targeted resilience-building interventions.

adaptation_focus: smallholder adaptation strategies and perceived adaptive capacity
indicators_measured: adaptation measures adopted; information sources; farm income; labor availability; land availability; perceived adaptive capacity
strengths_and_limitations: Strength: Integrates perceptions, barriers, and adaptive-capacity analysis using mixed methods. Limitation: Findings are based on one coastal commune and may not generalize broadly.
lessons_learned: Improved climate information, training, and institutional support are essential to strengthen farmers’ ability to implement adaptation measures.
